scope: Convert tactical postmortem observations into model volatility factors; no score prediction, WDL probability or betting advice.
input_files: 汇总/模型优化执行方案_20260622.md

factor_scale.magnitude: Recommended model adjustment strength on a 0.00 to 1.00 scale. It should modify distribution shape or floor/tail allocation, not raw team strength directly.
factor_scale.direction: increase means raise the named volatility layer; decrease means suppress it; mixed means split by match state.
factor_scale.application_stage: Apply after baseline xG and attack/defense ratings, before final Poisson/tail scoreline normalization.

draw_floor_tactical_triggers.evidence: Backtest diagnosis lists Belgium 1-1 Egypt, Iran 2-2 New Zealand, Spain 0-0 Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia 1-1 Uruguay and Belgium 0-0 Iran as draw-tail misses. Human summary specifically recommends a dynamic draw floor for early group-stage matches, acceptable points states, low tempo and strong defensive evidence.
draw_floor_tactical_triggers.model_note: Raise low-event and strategically acceptable draw mass without directly changing team attack or defense ratings. Cap at medium strength unless at least two tactical triggers are present and one is match-state related.
draw_floor_tactical_triggers.double_counting_guard: Do not add this on top of an already inflated market draw adjustment and a separate low xG total adjustment at full weight. If total xG is already lowered by tempo, use this factor mainly to redistribute 0-0/1-1 rather than reduce goals again.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Spain 0-0 Cape Verde and Belgium 0-0 Iran show compact blocks can convert favorite pressure into low-event score states.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use only when recent postmatch or tactics packet confirms compact low/mid block and box protection.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If team defense_score already includes clean-sheet form, apply as distribution-shape floor, not another defensive rating boost.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Cape Verde and Iran examples show disciplined blocks can survive pressure when card/discipline risk does not break structure.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Requires no active red-card/suspension/discipline warning from data or red-team packets.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Do not stack with event_volatility red-card risk in the same direction; discipline either supports draw floor or suppresses collapse tail.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Backtest summary flags group-stage first/second-round risk management and acceptable points states as draw-tail drivers.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use when standings context makes one point tactically acceptable for both, especially after both teams have avoided losing or when a favorite does not need to chase margin.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Keep separate from market calibration. This is a tactical game-state prior, not a market-implied draw price.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Belgium 0-0 Iran notes Beiranvand saves; Spain 0-0 Cape Verde first-round review credited keeper/blocks.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use as a cap on favorite conversion when goalkeeper state is verified in player_state or postmatch.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If goalkeeper player_state already raises defense, apply no more than half magnitude.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Human backtest summary points to low-event and slow-tempo draws being underweighted.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Redistribute mass toward 0-0, 1-1 and narrow one-goal states after xG construction.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If xG total has already been reduced for pace, do not reduce xG again; only alter draw covariance.

event_volatility_triggers.evidence: Backtest diagnosis says red cards, early goals, goalkeeper overperformance and late collapse were not strong enough tail multipliers. Examples include Canada 6-0 Qatar, Switzerland 4-1 Bosnia and Herzegovina, Netherlands 5-1 Sweden and Tunisia 0-4 Japan.
event_volatility_triggers.model_note: Apply as a variance/tail layer that redistributes probability into high-total, lopsided or chaotic score states. It should not blindly raise both teams' attack_score.
event_volatility_triggers.double_counting_guard: When an event is already reflected in player_state form or injury/suspension adjustment, use the larger of the two or half-weight both. This layer models distribution shape after an event, not the base likelihood of the event.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Backtest explicitly lists red cards as a tail multiplier; Belgium 0-0 Iran also shows red-card game-state distortion.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use discipline history, referee profile if available, and matchup stress. Red-card risk should widen scoreline tails and reduce confidence bands.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Do not also apply full defensive collapse penalty unless a red card actually occurred in postmatch mode.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia and Canada 6-0 Qatar show early favorite breakthrough can destroy low-block assumptions and expand 4+ goal tails.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Pre-match: encode as early-goal sensitivity. Live/postmatch: if early goal occurs, shift from median scoreline to ceiling/tail state.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If favorite_ceiling_tail also triggers from early goal, route early-goal magnitude there and keep event_volatility for residual chaos only.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Backtest and recent postmortems flag set-piece chains: Spain fourth via corner sequence, Cape Verde free-kick, Egypt corner header, New Zealand aerial threat.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Raise volatility when one team repeatedly produces corners/free-kicks or the opponent has aerial/second-ball weakness.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If set_piece_advantage already exists in matchup tactics, cap combined value at the higher factor plus 0.05.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Backtest lists goalkeeper overperformance; Belgium 0-0 Iran and Spain 0-0 Cape Verde are keeper/block examples.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Overperformance raises draw/underdog survival; underperformance raises collapse tail. Require player_state or postmatch signal.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Half-weight if goalkeeper form_status_1_5 is already outside neutral band.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Large misses such as Netherlands 5-1 Sweden, Tunisia 0-4 Japan and Canada 6-0 Qatar indicate post-event collapse tails were too thin.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use when early concession, tactical mismatch, cards, heat/fatigue or repeated set-piece failures combine. This is a nonlinear tail switch.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Do not apply from reputation alone. Require at least two supporting triggers or postmatch evidence.

favorite_ceiling_tail_triggers.evidence: Backtest diagnosis says strong favorites were directionally correct but large-win tails were too thin. Examples: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia, Netherlands 5-1 Sweden and Canada 6-0 Qatar.
favorite_ceiling_tail_triggers.model_note: Expand favorite 3+ and 4+ goal tail only when tactical dominance can plausibly survive beyond initial lead. Do not raise favorite win probability mechanically; redistribute within favorite-win scorelines.
favorite_ceiling_tail_triggers.double_counting_guard: Avoid stacking with baseline_strength, attack_score and market favorite premium. This factor is a conditional ceiling/tail switch, not another team-quality score.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia showed early pressure, Rodri control and counterpress preventing relief.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use when favorite can both create and immediately recover possession after clearances.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If pressing_adjustment is already high, use this as tail-shape only, not xG increase.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Spain scored early after a prior low-block failure; early goal forced Saudi Arabia out of damage-control structure.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Pre-match: encode sensitivity to favorite early goal. Live/postmatch: activate strongly once early goal occurs.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Share weight with event_volatility early_goal trigger; never apply both at full strength.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Backtest summary flags strong favorites and large-win tails; tactical files repeatedly show favorites with stronger substitution waves converting late pressure.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use when favorite can add equal-or-better attackers after 60 minutes and opponent depth is thin.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: If player_state already boosts substitutes, cap coach_substitution_risk and this trigger combined.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Canada 6-0 Qatar and Netherlands 5-1 Sweden examples indicate tactical/discipline collapse after pressure can create non-Poisson lopsided tails.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Activate when opponent has cards, repeated rest-defense failures, conceded early, or lost compactness.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Coordinate with event_volatility collapse trigger; if both fire, ceiling-tail receives the favorite-specific share and event_volatility handles residual total-goal variance.

trigger_definitions.evidence: Spain's Yamal/Oyarzabal/Rodri pattern shows favorites become more explosive when width and central control are both active.
trigger_definitions.model_note: Use when favorite has both wide-channel advantage and central-control advantage above threshold.
trigger_definitions.avoid_double_counting: Do not add full wide_channel_advantage plus central_control_advantage plus this trigger; use this only as interaction term.

minimum_trigger_count.draw_floor_tactical_triggers: Require at least two triggers; one must be tactical structure or game-state motivation.
minimum_trigger_count.event_volatility_triggers: Require one strong event trigger or two medium triggers before widening tails.
minimum_trigger_count.favorite_ceiling_tail_triggers: Require favorite strength gap plus at least two tactical triggers; early breakthrough can serve as one trigger.

model_integration: Apply tactical volatility factors to redistribute scoreline mass, not to overwrite baseline team strength.
For draw floor, add covariance between team goal counts and lift 0-0/1-1/2-2 only where total-goal context supports it.
For event volatility, widen distribution tails and increase uncertainty intervals.
For favorite ceiling tail, move mass from narrow favorite wins into 3+ and 4+ favorite-win tails when triggers are active.
Always report highest single scoreline separately from aggregate 1X2 leader.

double_counting_controls: Attack/defense ratings describe baseline team quality; these factors describe game-state distribution shape.
Player_state captures individual form, injuries, cards and goalkeeper state; volatility factors should be capped or half-weighted when using the same evidence.
Set-piece events should not be counted as both set_piece_advantage and event_volatility at full weight.
Market calibration should not erase local draw floor; if market adjustment already boosts draw, use tactical draw floor as confirmation only.
Early goal triggers in event_volatility and favorite_ceiling_tail are mutually aware and should share weight rather than stack.
handoff_to_model_agent: Add these factors as named post-baseline distribution modifiers in future match prediction JSON.
Expose which triggers fired, their evidence, total magnitude after caps and the scoreline-mass redistribution method.
Keep all future prediction reports clear that single most likely scoreline can differ from aggregate 1X2 direction.
Backtest these factors by group stage round and favorite strength band to avoid overfitting to 32-match sample.
red_team_challenge_points: Challenge sample size and overfitting, especially large-win examples.
Challenge whether draw floor is being applied to teams that actually need to chase points.
Challenge duplicate use of goalkeeper, set-piece and red-card evidence already stored in player_state.
Challenge favorite ceiling tail when favorite lacks confirmed T-75m attacking lineup or bench depth.
Challenge whether market deep-favorite signals are being treated as tactical evidence.
data_gaps: Referee profiles and card propensity are not consistently available.
Official T-75m lineups and bench quality are needed before full favorite-ceiling activation.
Set-piece event maps and corner/free-kick sequence counts are incomplete in older matches.
Goalkeeper post-shot data and same-source ratings are not complete across all teams.
Psychological collapse indicators are inferred from match state and events; require red-team review before strong pre-match use.

source_log.path: 汇总/模型优化执行方案_20260622.md
